    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go", "symbol": "applyFrontendDefaults"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/conversion.go", "symbol": "RegisterConversions"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON model versioning and migration system is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## Backend Schema Version Migration (Go)\n\n### Core Migration Infrastructure\nThe migration system is orchestrated through `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go` which exports the `Migrate()` function as the main entry point. The `Initialize()` function sets up the migrator singleton with required datasource provider dependencies. The migration.go file implements a comprehensive pipeline that:\n1. Applies frontend defaults (DashboardModel constructor defaults)\n2. Applies panel defaults (PanelModel constructor defaults)\n3. Executes sequential schema version migrations (v0 → v42)\n4. Cleans up the dashboard to match frontend getSaveModel behavior\n\n### Dashboard Schema Versions\nThe schema versions are defined in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go` which maintains a registry of all version migrations (v2 through v42) via the `GetMigrations()` function that returns a map of version-to-migration-function mappings. Version constants MIN_VERSION (0) and LATEST_VERSION (42) are defined here, along with the SchemaVersionMigrationFunc type and DataSourceInfoProvider interface for dependency injection.\n\n### Field-Level Migration Functions\nEach schema version (v2.go through v42.go) implements a specific migration function that handles field-level transformations:\n\n- **v2.go (V2)**: Migrates legacy services.filter structure to modern time and templating.list format\n- **v6.go (V6)**: Moves annotations from pulldowns array to structured annotations object\n- **v7.go (V7)**: Converts nav array to timepicker structure\n- **v14.go (V14)**: Migrates sharedCrosshair boolean property to graphTooltip integer property\n- **v16.go (V16)**: Complex migration that upgrades from row-based layout to modern grid-based layout, including panel ID assignment and grid position calculations\n- **v36.go (V36)**: Migrates datasource references from legacy string format to structured UID-based objects with type and apiVersion information\n- **v42.go (V42)**: Latest schema version\n\nMigration functions from v3-v5, v8-v13, v15, v17-v35, v37-v41 are no-op migrations that only update the schema version for consistency.\n\n### Migration Utilities\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migration_utils.go` provides shared utility functions for type-safe field extraction and conversion across all migrations:\n- GetStringValue(), GetBoolValue(), GetIntValue(), GetFloatValue() for safe field access\n- ConvertToFloat(), ConvertToInt() for flexible numeric type conversions supporting floats, ints, and string suffixes like \"700px\"\n- IsArray() for type checking\n\n### Error Handling\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/errors.go` defines:\n- MigrationError: Error type for migration failures with context about current version, target version, and function name\n- MinimumVersionError: Error for dashboards below the minimum migration support version (v0)\n\n### Frontend Defaults Application\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go` replicates the frontend DashboardModel and PanelModel constructor defaults, including:\n- Dashboard-level defaults (title: \"No Title\", tags: [], timezone: \"\", graphTooltip: 0, etc.)\n- Panel-level defaults (gridPos with x:0, y:0, h:3, w:6, targets with refId \"A\", transparent: false, etc.)\n- Structure normalizations (ensureTemplatingExists, ensureAnnotationsExist, sortPanelsByGridPos)\n\n### Datasource Utilities\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/datasource_utils.go` provides helper functions for datasource migration operations.\n\n## API Version Conversion (Go)\n\n### Conversion Registration\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/conversion.go` registers all API version conversions using Kubernetes API machinery patterns:\n- Converts between dashboard API versions: v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1\n- Implements bidirectional conversions (v0 ↔ v1, v0 ↔ v2alpha1, v0 ↔ v2beta1, etc.)\n- Uses conversion metrics middleware to track successful and failed conversions\n\n### Version-Specific Conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v0.go`: v0 API conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v1.go`: v1 API conversions (e.g., Convert_V1_to_V0, Convert_V1_to_V2alpha1)\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v2.go`: v2 API conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`: Specific v2alpha1 to v2beta1 conversion\n\n### Conversion Metrics\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/metrics.go` tracks dashboard conversion success and failure metrics with labels for source version, target version, and schema version.\n\n### Conversion Error Handling\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/errors.go` defines error types for API version conversion failures.\n\n## Database Schema\n\n### Dashboard Version Storage\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/dashboard_version_mig.go` implements database migrations that:\n- Create dashboard_version table with columns for dashboard_id, version, parent_version, restored_from, created_by, rest_restore_of, and data\n- Add indices for efficient querying by dashboard_id and unique (dashboard_id, version) pairs\n- Initialize dashboard versions from existing dashboard records\n- Support api_version column for versioning the API format\n\n## Metrics and Monitoring\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/metrics.go` defines Prometheus metrics:\n- MDashboardConversionSuccessTotal: Counter for successful dashboard conversions\n- MDashboardConversionFailureTotal: Counter for failed dashboard conversions\n\n## Migration Pipeline Summary\n\nThe complete migration pipeline for a dashboard JSON update follows this sequence:\n1. **Input validation**: Ensure dashboard is not nil, remove properties frontend never includes\n2. **Frontend defaults**: Apply DashboardModel constructor defaults\n3. **Panel defaults**: Apply PanelModel constructor defaults to all panels\n4. **Panel ID uniqueness**: Ensure all panels have unique IDs\n5. **Schema migrations**: Execute all version-specific migrations sequentially\n6. **Built-in annotations**: Add default built-in annotation query\n7. **Cleanup**: Remove internal properties and filter to match frontend getSaveModel behavior\n8. **Verification**: Confirm final schema version matches target\n\nThis architecture ensures consistency between frontend (TypeScript) and backend (Go) migrations, with comprehensive testing and metrics for monitoring conversion quality."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Dashboard JSON Model Version Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement dashboard JSON model versioning and migration: the dashboard version schema definitions, the DashboardMigrator, and the field-level migration functions that upgrade old dashboard JSON to the current schema version.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

## Summary

I've identified **56 Go source files** in the grafana/grafana repository that implement dashboard JSON model versioning and migration:

### Core Components:

1. **Backend Schema Version Migration** (41 files):
   - Main orchestrator: `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`
   - Migration registry: `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go` (defining LATEST_VERSION = 42)
   - Schema versions v2-v42: Each implements field-level migrations for dashboard transformations
   - Supporting utilities: migration_utils.go (GetStringValue, ConvertToFloat, etc.), errors.go, datasource_utils.go

2. **Key Migration Functions** (V2-V42):
   - **V2**: Legacy services.filter → time/templating migration
   - **V6, V7**: Annotation and timepicker restructuring
   - **V16**: Complex row-based → grid-based layout upgrade
   - **V36**: Datasource references (string → UID-based objects)

3. **API Version Conversion** (7 files):
   - `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/conversion.go`: Registers bidirectional conversions between v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1
   - Version-specific converters (v0.go, v1.go, v2.go, v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go)
   - Conversion metrics and error handling

4. **Frontend Defaults & Infrastructure**:
   - `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go`: Replicates frontend DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults
   - Metrics system for tracking conversion success/failure
   - Database migrations for dashboard version storage

The answer.json file at `/workspace/answer.json` contains the complete structured list of all files, key symbols, execution chain, and a detailed narrative explanation of the entire system.

{

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON model versioning and migration system is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## Backend Schema Version Migration (Go)\n\n### Core Migration Infrastructure\nThe migration system is orchestrated through `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go` which exports the `Migrate()` function as the main entry point. The `Initialize()` function sets up the migrator singleton with required datasource provider dependencies. The migration.go file implements a comprehensive pipeline that:\n1. Applies frontend defaults (DashboardModel constructor defaults)\n2. Applies panel defaults (PanelModel constructor defaults)\n3. Executes sequential schema version migrations (v0 → v42)\n4. Cleans up the dashboard to match frontend getSaveModel behavior\n\n### Dashboard Schema Versions\nThe schema versions are defined in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go` which maintains a registry of all version migrations (v2 through v42) via the `GetMigrations()` function that returns a map of version-to-migration-function mappings. Version constants MIN_VERSION (0) and LATEST_VERSION (42) are defined here, along with the SchemaVersionMigrationFunc type and DataSourceInfoProvider interface for dependency injection.\n\n### Field-Level Migration Functions\nEach schema version (v2.go through v42.go) implements a specific migration function that handles field-level transformations:\n\n- **v2.go (V2)**: Migrates legacy services.filter structure to modern time and templating.list format\n- **v6.go (V6)**: Moves annotations from pulldowns array to structured annotations object\n- **v7.go (V7)**: Converts nav array to timepicker structure\n- **v14.go (V14)**: Migrates sharedCrosshair boolean property to graphTooltip integer property\n- **v16.go (V16)**: Complex migration that upgrades from row-based layout to modern grid-based layout, including panel ID assignment and grid position calculations\n- **v36.go (V36)**: Migrates datasource references from legacy string format to structured UID-based objects with type and apiVersion information\n- **v42.go (V42)**: Latest schema version\n\nMigration functions from v3-v5, v8-v13, v15, v17-v35, v37-v41 are no-op migrations that only update the schema version for consistency.\n\n### Migration Utilities\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migration_utils.go` provides shared utility functions for type-safe field extraction and conversion across all migrations:\n- GetStringValue(), GetBoolValue(), GetIntValue(), GetFloatValue() for safe field access\n- ConvertToFloat(), ConvertToInt() for flexible numeric type conversions supporting floats, ints, and string suffixes like \"700px\"\n- IsArray() for type checking\n\n### Error Handling\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/errors.go` defines:\n- MigrationError: Error type for migration failures with context about current version, target version, and function name\n- MinimumVersionError: Error for dashboards below the minimum migration support version (v0)\n\n### Frontend Defaults Application\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go` replicates the frontend DashboardModel and PanelModel constructor defaults, including:\n- Dashboard-level defaults (title: \"No Title\", tags: [], timezone: \"\", graphTooltip: 0, etc.)\n- Panel-level defaults (gridPos with x:0, y:0, h:3, w:6, targets with refId \"A\", transparent: false, etc.)\n- Structure normalizations (ensureTemplatingExists, ensureAnnotationsExist, sortPanelsByGridPos)\n\n### Datasource Utilities\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/datasource_utils.go` provides helper functions for datasource migration operations.\n\n## API Version Conversion (Go)\n\n### Conversion Registration\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/conversion.go` registers all API version conversions using Kubernetes API machinery patterns:\n- Converts between dashboard API versions: v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1\n- Implements bidirectional conversions (v0 ↔ v1, v0 ↔ v2alpha1, v0 ↔ v2beta1, etc.)\n- Uses conversion metrics middleware to track successful and failed conversions\n\n### Version-Specific Conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v0.go`: v0 API conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v1.go`: v1 API conversions (e.g., Convert_V1_to_V0, Convert_V1_to_V2alpha1)\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v2.go`: v2 API conversions\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`: Specific v2alpha1 to v2beta1 conversion\n\n### Conversion Metrics\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/metrics.go` tracks dashboard conversion success and failure metrics with labels for source version, target version, and schema version.\n\n### Conversion Error Handling\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/errors.go` defines error types for API version conversion failures.\n\n## Database Schema\n\n### Dashboard Version Storage\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/dashboard_version_mig.go` implements database migrations that:\n- Create dashboard_version table with columns for dashboard_id, version, parent_version, restored_from, created_by, rest_restore_of, and data\n- Add indices for efficient querying by dashboard_id and unique (dashboard_id, version) pairs\n- Initialize dashboard versions from existing dashboard records\n- Support api_version column for versioning the API format\n\n## Metrics and Monitoring\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/metrics.go` defines Prometheus metrics:\n- MDashboardConversionSuccessTotal: Counter for successful dashboard conversions\n- MDashboardConversionFailureTotal: Counter for failed dashboard conversions\n\n## Migration Pipeline Summary\n\nThe complete migration pipeline for a dashboard JSON update follows this sequence:\n1. **Input validation**: Ensure dashboard is not nil, remove properties frontend never includes\n2. **Frontend defaults**: Apply DashboardModel constructor defaults\n3. **Panel defaults**: Apply PanelModel constructor defaults to all panels\n4. **Panel ID uniqueness**: Ensure all panels have unique IDs\n5. **Schema migrations**: Execute all version-specific migrations sequentially\n6. **Built-in annotations**: Add default built-in annotation query\n7. **Cleanup**: Remove internal properties and filter to match frontend getSaveModel behavior\n8. **Verification**: Confirm final schema version matches target\n\nThis architecture ensures consistency between frontend (TypeScript) and backend (Go) migrations, with comprehensive testing and metrics for monitoring conversion quality."
}
